sourceMovie.title: The Last House on the Left
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Last House on the Left works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Cape Fear
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Last House on the Left clicked for you, Cape Fear (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Last House on the Left?
faq.answer: Try Cape Fear, I Spit on Your Grave, Suburbicon—each shares DNA with The Last House on the Left while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Last House on the Left?
faq.answer: The Last House on the Left (2009) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Horror, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Last House on the Left worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
